input_row.title: Ensure that 'Users can register applications' is set to 'No'
input_row.description: Require administrators or appropriately delegated users to register third-party applications.
input_row.rationale: It is recommended to only allow an administrator to register custom-developed applications. This ensures that the application undergoes a formal security review and approval process prior to exposing Microsoft Entra ID data. Certain users like developers or other high-request users may also be delegated permissions to prevent them from waiting on an administrative user. Your organization should review your policies and decide your needs. Impact: Enforcing this setting will create additional requests for approval that will need to be addressed by an administrator. If permissions are delegated, a user may approve a malevolent third party application, potentially giving it access to your data.
input_row.audit: Audit from Azure Portal 1. From Azure Home select the Portal Menu 2. Select Microsoft Entra ID 3. Under Manage, select Users 4. Under Manage, select User settings 5. Ensure that Users can register applications is set to No Audit from PowerShell (Get-MgPolicyAuthorizationPolicy).DefaultUserRolePermissions | Format-List AllowedToCreateApps Command should return the value of False
input_row.remediation: Remediate from Azure Portal 1. From Azure Home select the Portal Menu 2. Select Microsoft Entra ID 3. Under Manage, select Users 4. Under Manage, select User settings 5. Set Users can register applications to No 6. Click Save Remediate from PowerShell $param = @{ AllowedToCreateApps = "$false" } Update-MgPolicyAuthorizationPolicy -DefaultUserRolePermissions $param Default Value: By default, Users can register applications is set to "Yes". References: 1. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/role-based-access- control/delegate-app-roles#restrict-who-can-create-applications 2. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity-platform/how-applications-are- added#who-has-permission-to-add-applications-to-my-azure-ad-instance 3. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/mcsb-governance- strategy#gs-6-define-and-implement-identity-and-privileged-access-strategy 4. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/mcsb-privileged- access#pa-1-separate-and-limit-highly-privilegedadministrative-users 5. https://learn.microsoft.com/en- us/powershell/module/microsoft.graph.identity.signins/get- mgpolicyauthorizationpolicy?view=graph-powershell-1.0
input_row.impact: Enforcing this setting will create additional requests for approval that will need to be addressed by an administrator. If permissions are delegated, a user may approve a malevolent third party application, potentially giving it access to your data.

gpt_response:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- Azure ARM APIs can query the property regarding application registration permissions.
- The audit mentions specific API/CLI commands (PowerShell) that can verify this attribute.
- The control involves access settings, which can be directly queried via the API.

**PROGRAM NAME:** azure_enentra_id_application_registration_disabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
The automated program will check the following:

- **Azure Resource Manager APIs or Azure CLI commands to use:** 
  - The command `Get-MgPolicyAuthorizationPolicy | Format-List` will be used.

- **Specific resources to enumerate:**
  - Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD) policies at the tenant level.

- **Exact configuration properties/settings to validate:**
  - The property `AllowedToCreateApps` should be `False` to ensure that users cannot register applications.

- **Clear Pass/Fail criteria with expected values:**
  - If `AllowedToCreateApps` is `False`, configuration is compliant (Pass).
  - If `AllowedToCreateApps` is `True`, configuration is non-compliant (Fail).

- **Example API endpoint or CLI command:**
  - PowerShell: `$policy = Get-MgPolicyAuthorizationPolicy` to retrieve the current application registration setting, followed by checking `$policy.DefaultUserRolePermissions.AllowedToCreateApps` for the value.
